steps.title: Identify the contrast in sensory development
steps.content:
Wolves at two weeks old have severely limited senses — they can smell but cannot see or hear.

The next clause immediately presents dogs with the opposite capability: they can see, hear, and smell.

This is a direct contrast between the two animals' sensory abilities at the same age.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
by contrast, directly marks the opposition between wolves' limited sensory abilities and dogs' full sensory abilities.

The other three lack this key component: instead of marking opposition, they suggest restatement (in other words,), examples (for instance,), or consequence (accordingly,).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
